meaning: n.(螺旋浆引起的)滑流,(行驶中车辆后的)气流,起带动作用的助力
examples: He left a host of other riders trailing in his slipstream.
The springs roil the waters they flow into in a phantasmal slipstream.
The calves meanwhile benefited by gliding in their mother's slipstream.
But suspect and banned organisations seem to be moving in the slipstream of genuine charities.
The most recent exhibition was a film project by David Cotterrell, titled Slipstream.
